content.problemStatement:
For a string SSS, we define f(S)f(S)f(S) as the number of **distinct palindromic†^{\dagger}† substrings‡^{\ddagger}‡** of SSS.

For example, f(abaa)=4f(abaa) = 4f(abaa)=4 because we can find the palindrome substrings a,b,aaa, b, aaa,b,aa and abaabaaba.

Call a string SSS good if f(S)≤5f(S) \le 5f(S)≤5, i.e. it has at most 555 distinct palindrome substrings.

You are given an integer NNN. Construct any good string of length NNN using only lowercase Latin characters. It can be proven that at least one good string exists. You are allowed to print any good string.

†^{\dagger}† A string is called palindromic if it reads the same forwards and backwards.

‡^{\ddagger}‡ A substring is a string that can be obtained by deleting some (possibly zero) characters from the beginning and some (possibly zero) characters from the end.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output a string SSS of length NNN, using only lowercase Latin characters, with f(S)≤5f(S) \le 5f(S)≤5.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : aaaaaa has 222 distinct palindrome substrings, aaa and aaaaaa.
Test Case 2 : f(abaa)=4f(abaa) = 4f(abaa)=4 as mentioned in the statement.
